title: Humanities annual updates 21-22
text:
College of the Siskiyous
Humanities
Program Review Annual Update
2021-22
Full time faculty in the Humanities discipline: Michael Tischler and Chris Vancil
Principal Preparer: Valerie Roberts, Interim Dean of Liberal Arts
1. Describe annual progress/changes/events in the program.
• Classes in the Humanities area continued to be taught remotely in fall 2020 and
spring 2021.
• The Humanities disciplines (HUM and PHIL) FTES from 2019-20 to 2020-21 are as
follows:
2019-20 2020-21
HUM 28.68 20.5
PHIL 11.12 2.75
o Fewer HUM 1001 sections were needed due to enrollment, and the classes
offered were slightly lower enrolled than in the previous year.
o HUM 1005, which has traditionally been a popular class, was not offered
because it is difficult for students who do not have reliable internet
connections.
o One of our two part-time philosophy instructors retired at the start of the
2020-21 year and the other taught only in the fall of 2020.
o No PHIL classes were offered in spring 2021.
• Students earned the following awards in the Humanities disciplines:
2020-21
Humanities AA 0
LAS Humanities AA 13
Philosophy AA 1
2. Describe progress on assessing SLOs and PLOs for the program.
Course assignments and grades are being consistently evaluated for student
understanding and learning, however without an institutional process, faculty have not
officially been assessing SLOs or PLOs.
3. Discuss any support or obstacles encountered by the program.
Support
• The Distance Learning Coordinator provided support to faculty.

-- 1 of 2 --

Obstacles
• As noted in the 2020-21 Annual Update, the elimination of the ENGL 0900 course, which
was a prerequisite to HUM 1001, has meant some students come in with a lower
skillset.
• Lower student enrollment in in HUM 1001 continued from last year (pandemic).
• Part time philosophy faculty not replaced.
4. Describe program budgetary needs or implications.
CQIPs needed?
• None noted.

-- 2 of 2 --
